notes: SHIP v1.14.272 - WET FINISH (RULING items 1+2+3 ONLY; item 4 DROPPED FOREVER). Owner: ".271 verified, ship wet". THIS IS THE CHANGE THE SCENE LOCK WAS HELD OPEN FOR - S44 re-opened the rack lock for THIS CHANGE ONLY and it RE-ARMS ON THE OWNER'S WET PASS. Nothing else in the scene rides along: no lights, no fog, no tone mapping, no tray geometry/internals, no type colours, no bezel strips, no camera (that was .271), no boot. VERIFIED IN THE DIFF: zero lines touching keyL/rimL/DirectionalLight/AmbientLight/HemisphereLight; the floor mesh is still MeshBasicMaterial; zero roughness/metalness/envMap/castShadow/receiveShadow anywhere in the floor or reflection block. ==== ITEM 1 - WET IS DARKER (the cue everyone forgets). Tile paint base 24,29,37 -> 20,25,31 (-15%), grout #090c11 -> #080a0e (scaled with it, staying JUST under the tile and never reaching the void #04060a - that collision is what made grout invisible before .267). This is AUTHORED PAINT: deterministic, no light involved. ==== ITEM 2 - REFLECTION PRESENCE UP. Ghost clone opacity 0.22 -> 0.32; tile plane opacity 0.80 -> 0.78. (The ruling doc said "0.85 -> 0.78" - 0.85 was the STALE .266 value; .267 already set 0.80. Corrected against live code.) BOUNDS HELD: satin-wet, NEVER a literal mirror - the tile grid must still read THROUGH the reflection everywhere. If seams vanish at the contact zone, clone opacity 0.32 is the number to back off. The bright-pool failure mode remains the HARD CEILING. ==== ITEM 3 - CONTACT-SHARP FALLOFF IS *THE* WET SIGNATURE. A dry floor reflects evenly; a wet one is bright where the object MEETS it and dissolves with distance. Implemented exactly as the ruling specced the lever: onBeforeCompile injecting a world-Y smoothstep into the clone's alpha - no geometry change, no Reflector pass, same clone+tile architecture. The clone is mirrored below floorY (scale.y=-1, position.y=2*floorY), so distance-from-contact = depth below floorY and far-from-contact is the rack's TOP. ==== DEVIATION, REPORTED AND ARITHMETIC-BACKED: FADE = 1.0*RH, NOT the ruling's ~1.5 rack-heights. The clone extends EXACTLY 1.00 rack-height below floorY (computed against the real dims: RH 16.32, floorY -8.21, rack top reflects to -24.58, span 16.37 = 1.00*RH). A 1.5*RH fade therefore leaves alpha 0.257 at the far end - nothing is "gone" anywhere, because the geometry ends at 1.0 before the fade completes. A 1.0*RH fade reaches 0.000 exactly at the rack top, which IS "gone within ~1.5 rack-heights" and is also the SAFER direction against the mirror/bright ceiling. The ruling's stated INTENT ("gone") and its stated NUMBER (1.5) could not both hold; intent won. ==== ONE SHARED onBeforeCompile REFERENCE for every clone material, deliberately: three's program cache keys on material.customProgramCacheKey(), which defaults to onBeforeCompile.toString(). A per-mesh closure would key uniquely and recompile a program PER TRAY - a real 55fps risk on ~20-40 trays. Shared ref => ~1 program per material type. The injection is also token-guarded: if '#include <begin_vertex>' or '#include <dithering_fragment>' is missing it leaves the shader untouched rather than emit a varying that is declared but never written. ==== PROVEN PRE-DEVICE IN AN ISOLATION HARNESS (rebuilt for this ship; the .267 harness did not survive). Same vendored r128, the real dims, the VERBATIM shipped _wetOnCompile, ONE synchronous render (no rAF - rAF never fires in a hidden automation tab), white bg + black box, readPixels back. Alpha by height, IDENTICAL for MeshBasic and MeshStandard (so the injection works on both shader families the clone contains): contact/rack-base 0.322 (= full clone opacity) > 3/4 0.271 > mid 0.161 (exactly the smoothstep midpoint) > 1/4 0.051 > far-end/rack-top 0.000 (GONE). Monotonic: true. Shader errors: none. Programs compiled: 2 total - ONE PER MATERIAL TYPE, confirming the shared-reference cache strategy works. This is the first pre-device proof of a floor change since the .267 harness, and it targets the exact thing that cannot be checked by reading: whether the injected GLSL compiles and whether the gradient actually runs the right way up. ==== NOT VERIFIED HERE - DEVICE ONLY: the composited LOOK. The harness proves the alpha MATH and the shader COMPILE; it does not prove the wet READ on glass, because the app's frame loop is rAF-driven and never runs in the automation tab. The floor's appearance is, by the owner's own standing rule, his eye. ?legacy=1 byte-identical by construction: every change is inside rackElevation_render3D, which legacy never reaches (the FLAT|3D toggle is _rehRd-gated and legacy has no #reh3dCanvasHost). Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform; three-stamp .271->.272. ==== JOHN iPHONE GATE - THIS RE-RUNS THE P0, FULL 360: [1] floor visibly DARKER than .271 and reads WET at a glance. [2] the rack stands sharply in the polish AT CONTACT and dissolves with distance. [3] tile seams read THROUGH the reflection everywhere, including the contact zone (if they vanish there, back off clone opacity 0.32). [4] FULL 360: no flare, no bright pool at ANY orbit angle, no dark wedge, no mint - the P0 re-passes. [5] REAR VIEW TOO - you judged from the rear last time; it must read wet from the back-panel side, not just the lit face. [6] 55+fps for 2min (the shader now runs on every clone material). [7] ON PASS: THE RACK SCENE LOCK RE-ARMS - the camera term stays OPEN per S47.
